info.title: DamascusTransit API
info.description:
Real-time transit tracking and fleet management — multi-tenant SaaS edition.

## Authentication

Most read endpoints are public but require an `?operator=<slug>` query parameter.
1. **POST /api/v1/auth/login** — exchange email/password for a JWT token
2. Include the token as `Authorization: Bearer <token>` on protected endpoints

Roles: `super_admin` · `admin` · `dispatcher` · `driver` · `viewer`

Tokens expire after 24 hours.

get.description: Health check endpoint.

Step 17 — verbose probe used by uptime monitors and load balancers.

Returns 200 only when DB and Redis are both reachable AND a position has
been received in the last 6 hours (overnight tolerance for Damascus).
Body always returns timing breakdowns regardless of status.

post.description: Receive and record an uncaught JS error from a browser client.
Authenticate user and return JWT token.
Self-service user registration. Creates a viewer-role account.
Initiate a password reset. Always returns 200 to avoid user enumeration.
Consume a time-limited reset token and set a new password.

get.description: Return the authenticated user's profile.

put.description: Update the authenticated user's profile (name and phone only).

post.description: Change the authenticated user's password.

get.description: List all active routes with stop counts.

schema.title: Response List Routes Api Routes Get

get.description: Get single route details.
List all active stops.

schema.title: Response List Stops Api Stops Get

get.description: Find nearest stops using PostGIS RPC.

schema.title: Response Find Nearest Stops Api Stops Nearest Get

get.description:
Return upcoming bus arrivals with ETA for a specific stop.

ETA is calculated from live vehicle GPS positions using Haversine distance
and current speed. Falls back to city-average speed (25 km/h) when a
vehicle is stationary or speed data is unavailable.
List all active vehicles with latest positions.

schema.title: Response List Vehicles Api Vehicles Get

get.description: Get latest vehicle positions only (lightweight endpoint).

schema.title: Response Get Vehicle Positions Api Vehicles Positions Get

get.description:
Server-sent events (SSE) stream of vehicle position updates.

Two delivery modes, transparent to the client:

* **Pub/sub (S3.4)** — when a live-bus backend is configured
  (``REDIS_PUBSUB_URL`` / ``LIVE_BUS_BACKEND``), the connection sends one
  snapshot then relays updates pushed by the ingest path. No per-client
  database polling and no in-process state, so any web process can serve any
  client and the service scales horizontally without sticky sessions.
* **Poll (legacy)** — with no backend configured (e.g. serverless without a
  TCP Redis), it falls back to the original 2-second Supabase poll, so the
  existing deployment is unchanged.
Returns current WebSocket connection statistics.
Get fleet statistics and real-time metrics.

schema.title: Response Get Fleet Stats Api Stats Get

get.description: Get per-driver performance metrics (public read access).

schema.title: Response Get Driver Stats Api Stats Drivers Get

get.description: Get detailed performance metrics for a single driver.

schema.title: Response Get Driver Detail Api Stats Drivers  Driver Id  Get

get.description: Get schedule for a route by day of week.

schema.title: Response Get Route Schedule Api Schedules  Route Id  Get

get.description: Get all unresolved alerts.

schema.title: Response Get Active Alerts Api Alerts Active Get

get.description:
Driver session bootstrap: my vehicle, its approval state, and route.

Added 2026-06-11 — the driver console previously had no way to learn
its vehicle code / route, so the header showed em-dashes forever.

post.description:
Driver SOS / incident report → creates a critical alert for dispatch.

Added 2026-06-11 — the driver console and Flutter app both POSTed here
but the endpoint never existed (silent failure). Uses the existing
alerts pipeline so incidents appear on the admin dashboard immediately.
Photo payloads are accepted but only persisted when storage is
configured (see migration 018 / incident photos bucket).
Report driver's current position (vehicle must be admin-approved).
Start a new trip for the driver (vehicle must be admin-approved).
End the driver's current trip.
Update passenger count for current trip.

get.description: List all users scoped to the current operator.

schema.title: Response List Users Api Admin Users Get

post.description:
Create a new user.

Roles: admin may create any non-super_admin account. A dispatcher
(operator staff) may ONLY create driver accounts — this implements the
operating model where the operator issues each driver's username and
password, and the admin later approves the vehicle itself.

get.description:
List all vehicles including inactive ones, scoped to current operator.

Logic fix (2026-06-11): this used to inner-join from
vehicle_positions_latest, so vehicles with no GPS fix yet (e.g. every
newly registered vehicle awaiting approval) were invisible to admins.
It now reads the vehicles base table and merges positions on top.

Optional `?approval=pending|approved|rejected|suspended` filter feeds
the admin approvals queue.

schema.title: Response List All Vehicles Api Admin Vehicles Get

post.description:
Register a new vehicle (bus / microbus / taxi).

Approval workflow (migration 019): vehicles registered by an operator
(dispatcher) start as `pending` and cannot operate until an admin
approves them on /admin/approvals.html. Vehicles created directly by an
admin are approved immediately (the admin IS the approver).

put.description: Update vehicle details (operator staff may maintain their own fleet).

delete.description:
Soft-delete: mark a vehicle decommissioned + inactive (audit-logged).

Hard deletes would orphan trips/positions history, so the row is kept.

post.description:
Assign vehicle to route and/or driver.

Assignment is allowed while the vehicle is still pending — the operator
links the driver (with their issued username/password) FIRST, and the
admin's approval afterwards is what authorises the vehicle to operate.
The driver must belong to the same operator as the vehicle. Omitted
fields are left unchanged; empty strings clear the assignment.

get.description: All routes (including inactive), scoped to the operator.

post.description: Create a route (business fields; geometry added later via GIS).

put.description: Update a route's business fields (fare, names, timing, active flag).

get.description: Number of vehicles awaiting approval (drives the sidebar badge).

post.description:
ADMIN ONLY — approve / reject / suspend a vehicle, or send it back to
pending (`resubmit`). Operators (dispatchers) cannot call this: the whole
point of the workflow is that the operator registers the vehicle + driver
credentials and a separate, higher-privileged admin authorises it.

Allowed transitions:
    pending   -> approved | rejected
    approved  -> suspended
    rejected  -> pending   (resubmit)
    suspended -> approved | pending (resubmit)
Every decision is written to audit_log.

get.description: Most recent audit-log entries (approvals, assignments, creations).
Get all alerts (resolved and unresolved), scoped to current operator.

schema.title: Response List All Alerts Api Admin Alerts Get

get.description: List trips with optional filtering, scoped to current operator.

schema.title: Response List Trips Api Admin Trips Get

get.description: Get fleet analytics overview for dashboard.
Get fleet utilization over the last 24 hours, bucketed by hour.
Get per-route performance based on completed trips in the last 7 days.
Get driver scoreboard based on completed trips in the last 30 days.
Get GPS position data for heatmap visualization.

post.description: Generate simulated GPS positions (admin JWT auth).

get.description: Export all vehicles as CSV.
Export trips from the last 30 days as CSV.
Export all alerts as CSV.
Export all drivers as CSV.
Export route performance (last 7 days) as CSV.

post.description: Send a test email to verify Resend integration is configured.

get.description:
Vercel Cron endpoint — generates simulated GPS positions on schedule.

Secured by CRON_SECRET env var. Add to vercel.json crons config.
The comparison is constant-time (hmac.compare_digest) to prevent a
byte-by-byte timing oracle on the secret.

post.description: Webhook for Traccar GPS position updates. Secured by HMAC signature.
Webhook for Traccar events (engine on/off, speeding, etc). Secured by HMAC.

get.description: Serve individual GTFS static feed files.
Download full GTFS static feed as a ZIP archive (Google Maps compatible).

GTFS-Realtime feed (VehiclePositions + TripUpdates + Alerts).

Returns a binary protobuf FeedMessage (GTFS-RT 2.0).
Cached in-memory for 15 seconds.
Falls back to JSON when gtfs-realtime-bindings is not installed.

GTFS-Realtime feed (VehiclePositions + TripUpdates + Alerts).

Returns a binary protobuf FeedMessage (GTFS-RT 2.0).
Cached in-memory for 15 seconds.
Falls back to JSON when gtfs-realtime-bindings is not installed.
Return the VAPID public key for Web Push subscription setup.

post.description:
Store a Web Push subscription.

Passengers associate subscriptions with stop IDs to receive arrival alerts.
Drivers receive dispatch and route-change notifications.

delete.description: Remove a Web Push subscription by endpoint URL.

post.description:
Broadcast a push notification to all matching subscribers (admin only).

Filter by role ('passenger', 'driver', or None for all).
Returns counts of sent/failed/skipped notifications.
Send a test push notification to all current admin subscriptions.

get.description: List all fleet operators (super_admin only).

schema.title: Response List Operators Api Operators Get

post.description: Register a new fleet operator (super_admin only).

get.description: Get the current user's operator profile.

post.summary: Telemetry frame (JSON form of telematics.proto VehicleStatus)
post.description:
JSON variant — handy for dev + integration tests. In production the
Protobuf endpoint is preferred for the 80% bandwidth saving.

Protobuf variant — production target.

Body decoding is currently a placeholder: we accept any opaque payload,
verify the HMAC, and return 204. Once the protoc-generated bindings
land in api/proto/telematics_pb2.py, this handler parses the body,
runs the same `_ingest` pipeline, and returns the same 204.

get.description:
Return the signed QR payload for a vehicle (driver/operator/admin).

The driver app renders this as a QR code; operators may also print it
as the permanent in-vehicle sticker. Only approved vehicles get a code.

post.description:
Passenger scanned a vehicle QR — create a pending fare payment.

Anti-tamper rules:
* QR signature must verify (counterfeit stickers rejected).
* When the vehicle's assigned route has a fixed fare (bus/microbus),
  the paid amount MUST equal the route fare — the client cannot
  manipulate the price. Taxis (no fixed fare) pass the metered amount.

get.description: Poll a payment's state (the UUID itself is the access capability).

post.description:
Sham Cash server-to-server confirmation callback (live mode).

HMAC-SHA256 over the raw body with SHAM_CASH_WEBHOOK_SECRET,
constant-time compared. Fails closed when the secret is unset.

SANDBOX ONLY — simulate the Sham Cash confirmation callback.

Disabled entirely in live mode. Lets the team demo the full passenger
payment loop before merchant credentials exist.

get.description: Operator-scoped payment ledger for the admin dashboard.

PaymentInitiateRequest.title: PaymentInitiateRequest
PaymentInitiateRequest.description: Passenger scanned a vehicle QR and wants to pay the fare.

ShamCashWebhookPayload.title: ShamCashWebhookPayload
ShamCashWebhookPayload.description: Callback body from Sham Cash (or the sandbox simulator).

VehicleApprovalRequest.title: VehicleApprovalRequest
VehicleApprovalRequest.description: Admin decision on whether a vehicle may operate.

VehicleAssign.title: VehicleAssign
VehicleAssign.description:
Either field may be omitted to leave it unchanged; explicit empty
string clears the assignment.

_PartialDecode.title: _PartialDecode
_PartialDecode.description:
JSON shape that mirrors telematics.proto VehicleStatus 1:1.
Names are snake_case to match the proto field names.

tags.name: health

tags.name: auth
tags.description: User authentication. Returns a JWT bearer token.

tags.name: stream
tags.description: Server-Sent Events (SSE) stream for live vehicle updates. Public.

tags.name: websocket
tags.description: WebSocket real-time vehicle tracking with route subscriptions.

tags.name: stats
tags.description: Aggregate fleet statistics. Public read access.

tags.name: schedules

tags.name: driver
tags.description: Driver-only endpoints. Requires `driver` role JWT.

tags.name: admin
tags.description: Admin and dispatcher endpoints. Requires `admin` or `dispatcher` role JWT.

tags.name: traccar
tags.description: Traccar GPS device webhooks. Secured by HMAC signature header.

tags.name: gtfs
tags.description: GTFS static and realtime feeds for Google Maps / transit apps.

tags.name: operators
tags.description: Fleet operator (tenant) management. super_admin role required for most operations.

tags.name: cron

tags.name: feedback
tags.description: Passenger feedback submission and driver rating summaries.

tags.name: payments
tags.description: Sham Cash fare payments — signed vehicle QR, initiate/status, HMAC-verified webhook, sandbox simulator. See docs/ARCHITECTURE_DECISIONS.md §10.

tags.name: telemetry
tags.description: Protobuf-over-HTTP bridge endpoints that accept the same wire format the MQTT broker will eventually deliver. Phase S2 of Scale_100k_Roadmap.md. Authenticated by HMAC device signature, NOT JWT.
